enrichment.summary: Kyle shares a vlog from Boulder on March 1, 2016, responding to viewer comments about his health after disclosing a possible cancer scare. He reflects on his sedentary lifestyle, weight gain (203 lbs), and chronic pain, then announces plans to get fit by becoming a 'bike office worker' — riding rollers while working — and organizing his own '24 Hours of Boulder' mountain bike event to eventually compete at a world-class level.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Taking it as a wake-up call rather than panicking; attributes poor health to general unhealthy lifestyle
Reconsidering bacon after learning it is linked to cancer
Endurance cycling (24-hour mountain biking) is the ideal overall fitness metric; being lean is a sign of health
Previously skipped exercise to maximize website work, but now believes he can do both simultaneously on a bike
Riding rollers while working is his ideal training method; targets 100 miles/day
Strongly values it; wants to model productive AND healthy living on camera

entities: Silvia Lannie (Boulder 24-hour racer who moved away)
